title: Myrsine arborea

intro: Myrsine arborea, also known as Añilillo or Guayacán, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Myrsine arborea include myrsinol, myricetin, and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, as well as to make tinctures and salves for topical application.
